denial_intake.denial_reason_text: Based on review of submitted clinical documentation, continued skilled nursing facility care is no longer medically necessary. The member has reached a plateau in functional improvement and skilled services are no longer required. Care can be safely transitioned to a lower level of care including home health services.
denial_intake.treating_physician: Dr. Helen Smith, MD (Geriatrics, Cedars Bay Medical Group)

symptoms: non-weight-bearing right lower extremity per orthopedic protocol
elevated fasting blood glucose (190-240 mg/dL) impairing wound healing
balance deficits with documented fall risk score of 18/25 (Morse)
fatigue limiting therapy tolerance to 30 minutes per session

treatment_history.event: ORIF of right tibial plateau fracture at Cedars Bay Hospital
Admitted to Pacific Oaks SNF for post-acute rehabilitation
Endocrinology consult; insulin regimen adjusted for SNF setting
Wound care specialist consult for slow incision healing attributed to diabetes

prior_treatment_attempts.attempt: Discharged home after prior left wrist fracture without SNF stay
prior_treatment_attempts.outcome: Re-injury within 9 days due to inability to safely manage ADLs alone; readmitted for second surgery

personal_evidence.treating_physician_statement: Patient is a 91-year-old female with insulin-dependent type 2 diabetes who is 22 days post ORIF of a complex right tibial plateau fracture. She remains strictly non-weight-bearing on the right leg per orthopedic protocol for a minimum of 6 weeks post-op. Skilled nursing services remain medically necessary for: (1) daily blood glucose management with sliding-scale insulin, which she cannot self-administer reliably given cognitive fatigue; (2) ongoing wound assessment given diabetic-related healing delay confirmed by wound care specialist; (3) supervised PT to maintain upper-body strength and contralateral lower-extremity conditioning while non-weight-bearing; (4) fall prevention given her documented Morse score of 18 and recent re-injury history after premature home discharge. Discharge to home or home health at this time is contraindicated and would predictably result in re-injury, as occurred after her November 2025 wrist fracture. Estimated additional SNF need: 14-21 days. — Helen Smith, MD

citations.source: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, CMS Pub 100-02, Chapter 8, §30.2
citations.quote: Skilled care may be necessary to improve a patient's current condition, to maintain the patient's current condition, or to prevent or slow further deterioration of the patient's condition.

citations.source: Jimmo v. Sebelius Settlement Agreement (2013), reaffirmed by CMS Transmittal 179 (2014)
citations.quote: Coverage of skilled nursing and skilled therapy services does not turn on the presence or absence of a beneficiary's potential for improvement, but rather on the beneficiary's need for skilled care.

An MA plan must provide coverage of, by furnishing, arranging for, or making payment for, all services that are covered by Part A and Part B of Medicare ... and must comply with CMS's National Coverage Determinations.

citations.source: CMS Final Rule CMS-4201-F (effective January 1, 2024), 88 FR 22120
citations.quote: MA organizations may not use internal coverage criteria that are more restrictive than traditional Medicare coverage criteria, and must base medical necessity determinations on the circumstances of the specific individual.

citations.source: UnitedHealthcare Medicare Advantage Coverage Summary: Skilled Nursing Facility Services (2026 edition)
citations.quote: Continued SNF coverage is appropriate when the member requires daily skilled nursing or rehabilitation services that, as a practical matter, can only be provided in a SNF, including patients with comorbid conditions such as diabetes mellitus that complicate post-orthopedic recovery.

citations.source: Estate of Lokken v. UnitedHealth Group, Case No. 0:23-cv-03514 (D. Minn. 2023)
citations.quote: Plaintiffs allege that UnitedHealthcare's nH Predict algorithm systematically denies and prematurely terminates post-acute care coverage in violation of Medicare coverage rules and the Jimmo settlement.

guidelines_found: American Academy of Orthopaedic Surgeons (AAOS) post-op tibial plateau fracture rehabilitation protocol — non-weight-bearing 6-8 weeks
American Diabetes Association Standards of Care 2026 §16: post-surgical glycemic management requires daily skilled monitoring
American Geriatrics Society Beers Criteria — falls risk management in post-fracture geriatric patients
cms_ncd_references: CMS Pub 100-02 Ch. 8 §30 (SNF skilled care criteria)

actions_taken: Requested complete medical records from Pacific Oaks SNF — received
Requested operative report and post-op orders from Cedars Bay Hospital — received
Requested letter of medical necessity from Dr. Smith — received and included in personal_evidence
Requested UnitedHealthcare's algorithmic review documentation under 42 CFR §422.568(f) — pending response (request submitted 2026-04-08, 30-day deadline 2026-05-08)
Searched CMS appeals database for similar nH Predict-related reversals at redetermination — 14 favorable matches identified

supplemental_sources.source: STAT News investigation, 'UnitedHealth uses AI model with 90% error rate to deny care' (2023-11-14)
supplemental_sources.result: Cited contextually; not used as primary evidence

supplemental_sources.source: Senate Permanent Subcommittee on Investigations report, 'Refusal of Recovery' (October 2024)
supplemental_sources.result: Documents UnitedHealthcare's elevated post-acute denial rates relative to traditional Medicare

additional_docs_retrieved: Pacific Oaks SNF nursing flow sheets (2026-03-15 to 2026-04-03)
Pacific Oaks SNF PT/OT progress notes
Patient's November 2025 readmission discharge summary (prior failed home discharge)
